entries.text: App is awesome with lots of data, ring is lightweight and comfortable, customer service described as helpful and seamless.
Rings don't last long, battery issues starting around 6 months, some users report the AI feature makes up fake data.
Across 101 mentions, users praise sleep tracking and app quality but complain about durability and accuracy.
Would not recommend, walking sensor is off, cannot detect cycling or driving correctly, wildly off from Google Fit.
Love my Ultrahuman ring air, gives awesome health and fitness insight, customer service is excellent.
One year review warning this is not a good product - long-term durability complaint.

Engadget rated the Ring Pro 8.9/10, praising better-looking titanium hardware, a feature-rich charging case (firmware-update recovery, 45-day case battery), and a data-rich app; main knocks were higher price than rivals and no major leap over the Ring Air.

Tech Advisor's Ring Pro review (Editors' Choice) praised solid tracking, a slick subscription-free app, and up to 15-day battery life, but flagged the $479/£429 price as expensive, the design as chunky, and noted software features are largely unchanged from the Ring Air.

Ultrahuman raised roughly Rs 400 crore (~$48M) in a Series C round to fund US re-entry with the redesigned Ring Pro, geographic expansion, and new wrist-based/home health products; total equity raised now exceeds $60M.
Coverage of Ultrahuman's smart ring returning to the US market after the Ring Air was previously blocked over an Oura patent dispute, with the redesigned Ring Pro clearing the way for re-entry.

Multiple app store reviews complain about a glut of ads pushing upgrades to the paid 'Ultrahuman X' tier priced around $15/month, unreliable background syncing, and inability to adjust blood glucose target zones.
Tom's Guide reported that the subscription-free Ultrahuman Ring Pro is officially launching in the US, framing it as a direct pricing/business-model challenge to Oura.

A July 2026 review piece described the Ring AIR as Ultrahuman's most consistent seller, crediting its detailed sleep, recovery, and heart-rate tracking plus the lack of a monthly subscription for its staying power against rivals.

Ultrahuman told Ring Pro Kickstarter backers and store customers that shipments are delayed again, now expected to begin August 10, citing extra quality-control checks during production scale-up. This is the second slip from the original June window, and backers voiced visible frustration on Reddit over the repeated delays.

Ultrahuman HYROX Bengaluru, India's first two-day HYROX event, drew a record 8,200+ participants and 12,000+ spectators, up sharply from prior India editions, with Olympic medallist P.V. Sindhu among the competitors.
The Ultrahuman Ring PRO Kickstarter campaign reached 1,227 backers and $473,400 pledged as of its July 2 update, reflecting continued demand despite the shipping delays.

A customer reported their first Ultrahuman ring had a battery fault, and after replacement the second unit failed within just 6 days, alongside complaints about slow 'priority support' response times spanning multiple escalations.
